你知道嗎？大部分人使用 ChatGPT 時，只用到它功能的一小部分，錯過了真正的 AI 力量！
其實，ChatGPT 裡有隱藏設定，可以讓它變得 10 倍更聰明、更貼近人性、也更實用，幫你處理日常工作、創作內容甚至專案管理。
就像你用 Google 搜尋時打開 SafeSearch 一樣，如果不調整設定，你永遠看不到最強大的功能。
只要 60 秒，你就能解鎖完整 ChatGPT 力量：
1️⃣ 打開設定，啟用「顯示額外模型」來使用最新功能
2️⃣ 開啟個人化，設定自訂指令，讓回應更自然貼近人類語氣
3️⃣ 啟用記憶功能，讓 ChatGPT 記住你的對話，隨時間更懂你
4️⃣ 在「應用程式與連結」中連接 Google Calendar、Gmail、Slack 等工具
5️⃣ 在資料控制中關閉「改善模型」，保護你的資料隱私

text:
Just found something really cool. There's a brand new AI tool called Omma, built by the team behind Spline, the platform that powers some of the best 3D design on the web.

The idea is simple. You describe what you want and Omma builds it for you. Interactive 3D scenes, websites, games, apps... all from a text prompt. No code. No 3D experience needed.

I started with a basic 3D Earth globe. Then I kept adding to it with prompts, daytime and weather controls, the Moon orbiting around it, a rocket launching from the surface. A few prompts later I had a full interactive solar system where you can hover over planets and see their details.

And here's what makes it different from other AI builders. Everything Omma generates is fully editable. Colors, lighting, camera angles, motion, geometry, you're not stuck with what the AI gives you. You can tweak everything and make it yours. When you're done, export the whole thing as real code and deploy it anywhere.

There's also a community tab where you can explore what other people have built and remix any project as your starting point. Some of the stuff in there is insane.

Try it yourself and let me know what you think!

text:
Chatgpt😭🥀
#chatgpt #color #fypppppppppppppp #fyp 

ChatGPT is an artificial intelligence chatbot created by OpenAI that can understand and generate human-like text. You can talk to it like you would with a person, and it will try to answer questions, explain topics, help with homework, write text, or even just chat.

It works using advanced AI models (called language models) that were trained on huge amounts of text. Because of that, it can recognize patterns in language and respond in a way that sounds natural and relevant.

People use ChatGPT for many things, for example:

Explaining school subjects (like math, history, science)

Writing essays, stories, or messages

Helping with coding or tech problems

Giving ideas, advice, or summaries

Even though it’s very useful, it’s not perfect. It doesn’t truly “think” or have opinions—it just predicts good responses based on data. So sometimes it can make mistakes or give outdated info.

In short: ChatGPT is a smart AI assistant you can chat with to get help, learn things, or create content.

text:
I actually recorded this video a week ago but wasn’t sure about posting it because I have a close friend working at OpenAI and honestly was worried about damaging our relationship. 

I decided to just check in with them and they were understanding. I say this because the point here isn’t that ChatGPT or OpenAI are evil. 

I just think it’s an inferior product right now because of how sycophantic it is relative to other AI and I thought it was important for you to know.

As I mentioned my personal favorite is Claude. It find it to have the right combination of intelligence, clarity, and honesty - but I experiment with the other ones on occasion.

The space is moving incredibly quickly, with new updates being pushed out every day. Since I made this video a week ago there have already been developments, including conflicts between Claude and the US government and announcements of the Department of War working with OpenAI and such. I may comment on those developments but they don’t really change their original thesis in my video.

For the average user it’s important for you to use AI that is not sycophantic and right now ChatGPT is just not that, even when given custom instructions. 

I do hope that they make the changes needed to make the product what it needs to be - they were the OG.

Claude just published an important feature that makes it easy to import and migrate from ChatGPT to Claude so if you google Claude import you should be able to find it.

As I mentioned before I’m not sponsored by any of these organizations. It’s not an endorsement; it’s just me sharing my experience. 

Try the different models and see for yourself. We’re starting to talk to AI more than people - so just like you should be selective about your friends - prioritizing people who tell you what you need to hear, not just want to hear - be selective about your AI companion.

#chatgpt #claude #openai #anthropic

6 ChatGPT prompts that feel illegal to know 🤫

1. Master a Role Quickly (Even With No Experience)
Prompt:
“I want to land a role as a [job title] with little to no direct experience. Break down the most important 20% of skills, knowledge, and behaviours I need to focus on to outperform most candidates.”

2. Turn Your Background Into ‘Relevant Experience’
Prompt:
“I’m applying for [job role] but don’t have formal experience. Help me reframe my past jobs, education, and real-life situations into strong, relevant examples for interviews.”

3. Interview Like a Top 1% Candidate
Prompt:
“Act as a hiring manager for a [job role]. Ask me the most common and hardest interview questions, then critique my answers, rewrite them, and show me how a high-performing candidate would respond.”

4. Fix Weak Answers Before the Interview
Prompt:
“Here are my draft interview answers for [job role]. Identify where I sound inexperienced or unclear and upgrade my responses so they sound confident, structured, and hire-ready.”

5. Make Your Answers Impossible to Forget
Prompt:
“I’m interviewing for [job role]. Turn my experience into clear stories, frameworks, or examples that make my answers memorable and easy for interviewers to repeat afterwards.”

6. Use Rejection to Get the Next Offer
Prompt:
“I was rejected for a [job role]. Analyse the most likely reasons, identify gaps, and create a 30-day improvement plan so I come back as a stronger candidate.”
